en.extract:
Chapin's apalis (Apalis chapini) is a species of bird in the family Cisticolidae. It is found in Malawi, Tanzania, and Zambia. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forest.
The common name and the Latin binomial commemorate the American ornithologist James Chapin.

'''Chapin's apalis''' ('''''Apalis chapini''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Cisticolidae]]. It is found in [[Malawi]], [[Tanzania]], and [[Zambia]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]].

The common name and the Latin binomial commemorate the American ornithologist [[James Chapin]].<ref>{{cite book|last=Beolens|first=Bo|title=Whose Bird? Men and Women Commemorated in the Common Names of Birds|year=2003|publisher=Christopher Helm|location=London|page=81|author2=Watkins, Michael}}</ref>
